document.heading: STEER Clarification Emergency Amendment Act of 2026

To amend, on an emergency basis, the Strengthening Traffic Enforcement, Education, and Responsibility ("STEER") Amendment Act of 2024 to modify its applicability clause to correctly reflect its funding status with respect to the District's Intelligent Speed Assistance Program.
document.text: BE IT ENACTED BY THE COUNCIL OF THE DISTRICT OF COLUMBIA, That this act may be cited as the "STEER Clarification Emergency Amendment Act of 2026".

section.text: Section 8(d) of Strengthening Traffic Enforcement, Education, and Responsibility ("STEER") Amendment Act of 2024, effective April 20, 2024 (D.C. Law 25-161; 71 DCR 2248), is amended to read as follows:

para.text: With respect to the Ignition Interlock Device established by section 10a of the District of Columbia Traffic Act, 1925, effective April 3, 2001 (D.C. Law 13-238; D.C. Official Code § 50-2201.05a), as of January 1, 2027; and

For all other purposes, including the Intelligent Speed Assistance Program established by section 10a-1 of the District of Columbia Traffic Act, 1925, effective April 20, 2024 (D.C. Law 25-161; D.C. Official Code § 50-2201.05a-1), as of the effective date of the STEER Clarification Emergency Amendment Act of 2026, passed on emergency basis on February 3, 2026 (Enrolled version of B26-583).
Sections 4(d) and (f), 5, and 6 shall apply as of January 1, 2027.

section.codify:annotation: Section 2 of D.C. Act 26-273 provided that the amendments made to this section by section 3(a) of D.C. Law 25-161 shall apply as of March 4, 2026. Therefore those amendments have been implemented.
Section 2 of D.C. Act 26-273 provided that the amendments made to this section by section 3(b) of D.C. Law 25-161 shall apply as of March 4, 2026. Therefore those amendments have been implemented.
Section 2 of D.C. Act 26-273 provided that the amendments made to this section by section 3(c) of D.C. Law 25-161 shall apply as of March 4, 2026. Therefore those amendments have been implemented.
Section 2 of D.C. Act 26-273 provided that the amendments made to this section by section 3(d) of D.C. Law 25-161 shall apply as of March 4, 2026. Therefore those amendments have been implemented.
Section 2 of D.C. Act 26-273 provided that the amendments made to this section by section 3(e) of D.C. Law 25-161 shall apply as of March 4, 2026. Therefore those amendments have been implemented.

Section 2 of D.C. Act 26-273 provided that the amendment made to paragraph (4) of subsection (a) of this section by section 3(f) of D.C. Law 25-161 shall apply as of January 1, 2027. Therefore that amendment shall be implemented for this section on January 1, 2027. Furthermore, Section 2 of D.C. Act 26-273 provided that, with respect to the Ignition Interlock Program, that portion of subsection (b) of this section shall apply as of January 1, 2027. All other amendments made to this section by section 3(f) of D.C. Law 25-161 have been implemented and are effective from March 4, 2026.

section.heading: Fiscal impact statement.
section.text: The Council adopts the fiscal impact statement of the Budget Director as the fiscal impact statement required by section 4a of the General Legislative Procedures Act of 1975, approved October 16, 2006 (120 Stat. 2038; D.C. Official Code § 1-301.47a).

section.heading: Effective Date.
section.text: This act shall take effect following approval by the Mayor (or in the event of veto by the Mayor, action by the Council to override the veto), and shall remain in effect for no longer than 90 days, as provided for emergency acts of the Council of the District of Columbia in section 412(a) of the District of Columbia Home Rule Act, approved December 24, 1973 (87 Stat. 788; D.C. Official Code § 1-204.12(a)).
